title: DivergenceVanishing
summary: States the weighted divergence-vanishing interface corresponding to the parity input used in Lemma 4.1.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: States the weighted divergence-vanishing interface corresponding to the parity input used in Lemma 4.1.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Renormalization/DivergenceVanishing.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Analytic and functorial data for renormalized invariant integrals on a PE space. Its mathematical data are: normal Form: Poincare Normal Form Data. u, v, w X; poincare Einstein: Is Poincare Einstein normal Form; cutoff Data: for every I : Scalar Invariant, Curvature Integral Cutoff Data X I; cutoff Data normal Form: for every I : Scalar Invariant, cutoff Data I.normal Form equals normal Form; RInt: Scalar Invariant maps to Real; RInt eq phase B: for every I : Scalar Invariant, RInt I equals Renormalized Curvature Integral cutoff Data I; convergent Integral: Scalar Invariant maps to Real; linear qsmul: for every c : Rat I : Scalar Invariant, RInt Scalar Invariant.qsmul c I equals c times RInt I; divergence zero: Even X.bulk Dim maps to for every F : One Form Invariant, 2 minus X.bulk Dim : Int < equals F.1.weight maps to RInt div Invariant F equals 0; modulo Divergence eq: for every I J : Scalar Invariant, Invariant Evaluation Model.Equal Modulo Divergence I J maps to RInt I equals RInt J; associated integral eq: for every I : Scalar Invariant Itilde : Ambient Scalar Invariant, Scalar Straightenable I Itilde maps to RInt I equals RInt restrict To Einstein Slice Itilde; critical weight converges: for every I : Scalar Invariant, I.1.weight equals - X.bulk Dim : Int maps to Is Scalar Conformal Invariant I maps to RInt I equals convergent Integral I.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{normalForm}&\colon \operatorname{PoincareNormalFormData}.\operatorname{}{u, v, w} X\\\operatorname{poincareEinstein}&\colon \operatorname{IsPoincareEinstein} \operatorname{normalForm}\\\operatorname{cutoffData}&\colon \forall I \colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant}, \operatorname{CurvatureIntegralCutoffData} X I\\\operatorname{cutoffData\_normalForm}&\colon \forall I \colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant}, (\operatorname{cutoffData} I).\operatorname{normalForm} = \operatorname{normalForm}\\\operatorname{RInt}&\colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant} \longrightarrow \mathbb{R}\\\operatorname{RInt\_eq\_phaseB}&\colon \forall I \colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant}, \operatorname{RInt} I = \operatorname{RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral} (\operatorname{cutoffData} I)\\\operatorname{convergentIntegral}&\colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant} \longrightarrow \mathbb{R}\\\operatorname{linear\_qsmul}&\colon \forall (c \colon \mathbb{Q}) (I \colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant}), \operatorname{RInt} (\operatorname{ScalarInvariant}.\operatorname{qsmul} c I) = c \cdot \operatorname{RInt} I\\\operatorname{divergence\_zero}&\colon \operatorname{Even} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} \longrightarrow \forall F \colon \operatorname{OneFormInvariant}, 2 - (X.\operatorname{bulkDim} \colon \mathbb{Z}) <= F.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{weight} \longrightarrow \operatorname{RInt} (\operatorname{divInvariant} F) = 0\\\operatorname{moduloDivergence\_eq}&\colon \forall I J \colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant}, \operatorname{InvariantEvaluationModel}.\operatorname{EqualModuloDivergence} I J \longrightarrow \operatorname{RInt} I = \operatorname{RInt} J\\\operatorname{associated\_integral\_eq}&\colon \forall (I \colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant}) (\operatorname{Itilde} \colon \operatorname{AmbientScalarInvariant}), \operatorname{ScalarStraightenable} I \operatorname{Itilde} \longrightarrow \operatorname{RInt} I = \operatorname{RInt} (\operatorname{restrictToEinsteinSlice} \operatorname{Itilde})\\\operatorname{critical\_weight\_converges}&\colon \forall I \colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant}, I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{weight} = -(X.\operatorname{bulkDim} \colon \mathbb{Z}) \longrightarrow \operatorname{IsScalarConformalInvariant} I \longrightarrow \operatorname{RInt} I = \operatorname{convergentIntegral} I\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The renormalized integral supplied by the package. In explicit terms, for x of type pespace, and i of type scalar invariant, its defining expression is P.RInt I.
correspondence.leanType: def renormalizedIntegral (X : PESpace) [InvariantEvaluationModel] [P : PERenormalizationPackage X] (I : ScalarInvariant) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The package agrees with the finite part defined in Phase B. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem renormalizedIntegral_eq_phaseB (X : PESpace) [InvariantEvaluationModel] [P : PERenormalizationPackage X] (I : ScalarInvariant) : renormalizedIntegral X I = RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral (P.cutoffData I)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Lemma 4.1 for a natural divergence carrying the required weight witness. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem renormalizedIntegral_naturalDivergence_eq_zero (X : PESpace) [InvariantEvaluationModel] [P : PERenormalizationPackage X] (hn : Even X.bulkDim) (I : ScalarInvariant) (hDiv : NaturalDivergenceAtWeight I (2 - (X.bulkDim : Int))) : renormalizedIntegral X I = 0

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
